name: edge-case
task: Refactor this job status type to be more type-safe. Currently it uses a single interface with optional fields: `{ status: string; data?: any; error?: string; startedAt?: Date; completedAt?: Date }`. Make impossible states unrepresentable.
assertions: Response converts the single interface into a discriminated union type with a `status` string literal discriminant for each state
Response ensures the `data` field only exists on the completed/success variant, not on error or pending variants
Response ensures the `error` field only exists on the failed variant, not on success or pending variants
Response does not use `any` for the data field — it uses `unknown`, a generic type parameter, or a concrete type
Response uses `type` (not `interface`) for the union, since discriminated unions are type aliases by nature
